title: Divide by 4
strategies: Halve the number, then halve again (÷4 = ÷2 ÷2).

learn.title: Sharing Into 4 Equal Groups
learn.content: When you divide by 4, you share a total equally among 4 groups. Example: 24 counters shared into 4 groups → each group gets 6. So 24 ÷ 4 = 6.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.5 'Divide by 4' pp.281-284
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Division Facts: Divide by 4
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Halve Twice Strategy
learn.content:
Dividing by 4 is the same as halving twice. Try with 20:
Step 1: 20 ÷ 2 = 10 (first half)
Step 2: 10 ÷ 2 = 5 (half again)
Result: 20 ÷ 4 = 5.
This works because 4 = 2 × 2.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.5 'Divide by 4' pp.281-284
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Division Facts: Divide by 4
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Relate to ×4 Multiplication
learn.content: Draw 4 rows of 9 dots — that is a 4×9 array showing 36 total. Reading it as division: 36 dots in 4 rows means 36 ÷ 4 = 9 dots per row. Every ×4 fact gives a ÷4 fact.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.5 'Divide by 4' pp.281-284
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Division Facts: Divide by 4
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Number Line: Backward Jumps of 4
learn.content: To find 36 ÷ 4, start at 36 on a number line and hop backward by 4 each time: 36 → 32 → 28 → 24 → 20 → 16 → 12 → 8 → 4 → 0. Count the hops: 9 hops. So 36 ÷ 4 = 9.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.5 'Divide by 4' pp.281-284
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Division Facts: Divide by 4
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 절반 두 번 루틴 — ÷4 빠른 전략
learn.content: ÷4 = ÷2 두 번. 4 = 2 × 2이니까 4로 나눔 = 2로 두 번 나눔. 예: 24 ÷ 4 → 24 ÷ 2 = 12 → 12 ÷ 2 = 6 → 답 6. 예: 32 ÷ 4 → 32 ÷ 2 = 16 → 16 ÷ 2 = 8 → 답 8. 단계 1) 첫 번째 절반(÷2). 단계 2) 두 번째 절반(다시 ÷2). 팁: 머릿속에서 두 번 절반 구하기는 한 번 ÷4보다 쉬움.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.5 'Divide by 4' pp.281-284
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Division Facts: Divide by 4
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.7 Lesson 7.5 'Divide by 4' pp.281-284
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic F — Division Facts: Divide by 4
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: ÷4 검증 — 답 × 4 = 원래 수?
learn.content: ÷4 답을 구한 뒤 검산: 답 × 4 = 원래 수? 예: 28 ÷ 4 = 7 → 검산: 7 × 4 = 28 ✓ (= 7 × 2 × 2 = 14 × 2). 예: 36 ÷ 4 = 9 → 검산: 9 × 4 = 36 ✓. 🔍 검증: 답 × 4 = 답 더블의 더블 (예: 9 → 18 → 36). 흔한 실수: 절반 두 번 하면서 한 번 빼먹어 답이 두 배 큼.

feedback.correct: Yes! 4 × 10 = 40, so each row has 10 flowers.
feedback.incorrect: Halve 40 twice: 40÷2=20, then 20÷2=10. So 40 ÷ 4 = 10 flowers per row.

try.feedback_correct: Yes! 4 × 10 = 40, so each row has 10 flowers.

practice_r2.question: Find the difference: 802 − 348.
practice_r2.explanation: 802−348: 2−8 borrow → 12−8=4; tens 9−4=5 (after lending across zero); hundreds 7−3=4. Result: 454.

hints: Step 1: divide 40 candies into 4 jars — how many per jar?
Step 2: subtract 2 from each jar, then multiply by 4 jars.

feedback.correct: Yes! 40÷4=10 per jar, 10−2=8, and 8×4=32 candies remain.
feedback.incorrect: 40÷4=10 per jar. 10−2=8 per jar. 8×4=32 total remain.

practice_r3.question: Tim has 40 candies. He splits them equally into 4 jars, then eats 2 from each jar. How many candies remain in total?
practice_r3.feedback_correct: Yes! 40÷4=10 per jar, 10−2=8, and 8×4=32 candies remain.

hints: Step 1: if ? ÷ 4 = 6, multiply back: 4 × 6 = ? to find the mystery number.
Step 2: once you know the mystery number, multiply it by 2.

feedback.correct: Correct! 4×6=24 is the mystery number, and 24×2=48.

practice_r3.question: A mystery number ÷ 4 = 6. What is the mystery number × 2?
practice_r3.feedback_correct: Correct! 4×6=24 is the mystery number, and 24×2=48.

practice_r3.feedback_correct: Yes! 36÷4=9 per page. 3 pages × 9 = 27 stickers remain.
